tree.setup: Your friend calls to make weekend plans.
tree.setup_zh: 你的朋友打电话来安排周末活动。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 周末有什么安排吗？要不我们一起出去玩？
turns.text_pinyin: zhōumò yǒu shénme ānpái ma? yàobù wǒmen yīqǐ chūqù wán?
turns.text_en: Any plans this weekend? How about we go out together?

options.text_zh: 好啊！除了星期天下午，我都有空。你觉得去哪儿合适？
options.text_pinyin: hǎo a! chúle xīngqītiān xiàwǔ, wǒ dōu yǒu kòng. nǐ juéde qù nǎr héshì?
options.text_en: Great! I'm free except Sunday afternoon. Where do you think is good?
options.feedback: Perfect — 除了...都 narrows your availability, and 合适 asks for their opinion naturally.

options.text_zh: 好啊，周末应该有空。去哪里好呢？
options.text_pinyin: hǎo a, zhōumò yīnggāi yǒu kòng. qù nǎlǐ hǎo ne?
options.text_en: Sure, I should be free this weekend. Where should we go?
options.feedback: Enthusiastic but vague — 应该有空 is less useful than narrowing with 除了...都. Asking 去哪里 is fine but offering 合适 invites their opinion more naturally.

options.text_zh: 这个周末我还没想好，你先安排吧。
options.text_pinyin: zhège zhōumò wǒ hái méi xiǎnghǎo, nǐ xiān ānpái ba.
options.text_en: I haven't decided yet this weekend, you plan it.
options.feedback: Deflects the planning entirely. Friends expect engagement — share your availability and contribute with 除了...都.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 要不去那个新开的咖啡馆？听说还不错。我约了小李，你们认识吧？
turns.text_pinyin: yàobù qù nàge xīn kāi de kāfēiguǎn? tīngshuō hái búcuò. wǒ yuē le xiǎo lǐ, nǐmen rènshi ba?
turns.text_en: How about that new café? I heard it's good. I invited Xiao Li — you know each other, right?

options.text_zh: 认识！好的，那我们约星期六上午怎么样？我来定时间。
options.text_pinyin: rènshi! hǎo de, nà wǒmen yuē xīngqīliù shàngwǔ zěnmeyàng? wǒ lái dìng shíjiān.
options.text_en: Yes! OK, how about Saturday morning? I'll set the time.
options.feedback: Natural — 约 for making plans, 我来定 shows initiative without being bossy.

options.text_zh: 行，听你们安排就好，我都可以。
options.text_pinyin: xíng, tīng nǐmen ānpái jiù hǎo, wǒ dōu kěyǐ.
options.text_en: Sure, I'll go along with whatever you plan, I'm flexible.
options.feedback: Agreeable but entirely passive — suggesting a time with 约 and taking initiative with 我来定 shows real investment in the plan.

options.text_zh: 我不太想去那个咖啡馆，没什么兴趣。
options.text_pinyin: wǒ bù tài xiǎng qù nàge kāfēiguǎn, méi shénme xìngqù.
options.text_en: I don't really want to go to that café, not very interested.
options.feedback: Rejecting without suggesting an alternative feels awkward. If declining, offer another idea with 要不我们去...

tree.cultural_note: 约 (yuē) is the core verb for making plans with friends. 要不 (yàobù) is a casual way to suggest alternatives. Chinese friendships often involve group coordination — being flexible but decisive (我来定) is appreciated.
